Title: A Maize Gene Regulatory Network for Phenolic Metabolism

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Name: PAL1; PAL3; PAL4; PAL5; PAL6; PAL8; PAL9
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamate, initiating phenylpropanoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for lignin and flavonoid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant structural support, defense, environmental interactions, pigmentation-related specialized metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Serves as key entry-point biosynthetic genes whose promoters are major nodes in the maize phenolic regulatory network
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Multiple PAL promoters were used as Y1H baits to uncover TF binding architecture of maize phenolic metabolism. 2. Seven PAL promoters (PAL1, PAL3, PAL4, PAL5, PAL6, PAL8, PAL9) were bound by MYB65. 3. PAL4 promoter was strongly transactivated by MYB65 in maize protoplasts, supporting active transcriptional control of PAL genes. 4. PAL5 promoter was bound in vivo by MBF1.3 in ChIP-qPCR.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast one-hybrid promoter screens; directed Y1H; ChIP-qPCR for selected promoter-TF pairs; transient luciferase transactivation for PAL4
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key pathway entry step; rate-limiting status not directly established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seedling protoplast assays from etiolated B73 × Mo17 F1 leaves; greenhouse/growth chamber growth conditions
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native maize PAL promoters used as bait fragments (~0.5–2 kb upstream regions); 35S constitutive promoter used for TF effectors in validation assays
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: CAGE for transcription start site definition; RNA-seq referenced for integrated regulatory interpretation; no accession provided in excerpt
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MYB65; MBF1.3; multiple additional TFs from Y1H network
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promoter/gene nodes useful for engineering broad phenylpropanoid flux and lignin/flavonoid traits through upstream transcriptional control
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: These genes are pathway targets/promoter baits rather than individually functionally manipulated enzymes in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: General phenylpropanoid pathway feeding lignin and flavonoids
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates hydroxycinnamic acids such as 4-coumarate to corresponding CoA thioesters
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate activated precursors for lignin and flavonoid branches
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense, structural polymers, pigmentation, environmental adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a central early phenylpropanoid node under dense TF control in the maize phenolic GRN
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. 4CL3 was highlighted as a key early phenolic gene in integrated GRN analysis. 2. KN1, P1, and FEA4 bind 4CL3 in integrated ChIP/Y1H networks. 3. P1-4CL3 interaction was identified by both Y1H and ChIP-seq. 4. MYB100, MBF1.3, and DOF28 showed ChIP evidence of association with 4CL3 despite some interactions not detected by Y1H. 5. Numerous feed-forward loops converge on 4CL3.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; directed Y1H; ChIP-qPCR; integration with published ChIP-seq and RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key early control node in network; rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native maize 4CL3 promoter bait; 35S constitutive promoter used for TF fusion constructs in ChIP validation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: CAGE for TSS definition; published ChIP-seq and RNA-seq integrated, no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: P1; KN1; FEA4; MYB100; MBF1.3; DOF28; JMJ13; C3H42; ARF34; EREB
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate network node for modifying total phenylpropanoid flux through regulatory engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as one of two example-centered subnetworks to illustrate feed-forward loops and network motifs.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers hydroxycinnamoyl groups from hydroxycinnamoyl-CoA to shikimate/quinate, generating hydroxycinnamoyl shikimate intermediates
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms intermediate precursors for monolignol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall reinforcement and structural support
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Important lignin-pathway targets with high TF connectivity in the network
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB100 binding to HCT6 was identified by Y1H and validated by ChIP-qPCR. 2. DOF28 binding to HCT10 was identified by Y1H and validated by ChIP-qPCR. 3. HCT6 and HCT10 showed among the highest normalized incoming TF connectivity of all promoters tested.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Network bottleneck-like highly connected promoters; enzymatic rate-limiting role not established
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native maize HCT6 and HCT10 promoters used as baits; 35S promoter used for TF fusion constructs
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MYB100; DOF28; C3H42; LBD35 and other Y1H TFs
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful lignin engineering targets because they are highly connected transcriptional nodes
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Promoter connectivity, rather than direct enzyme perturbation, is the focus of this paper.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Monolignol branch affecting G/S lignin composition
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: O-methylates lignin precursors in the pathway contributing to guaiacyl/syringyl monolignol formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Caffeic acid/caffeoyl derivatives and related lignin intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Ferulic acid/feruloyl derivatives and related methylated lignin intermediates
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Controls conversion of lignin precursors and influences lignin composition
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Structural polymer, cell wall integrity, stress adaptation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A major late lignin-gene regulatory hub extensively targeted by TFs; important for G- to S-lignin precursor conversion
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. COMT1 promoter was recognized by MYB100 in Y1H and confirmed by ChIP-qPCR. 2. COMT1 was the promoter with the highest incoming connectivity reported (161 TFs), partly due to deeper screening. 3. Integrated GRN analysis identified activation by KN1, FEA4, and P1, and repression by MYB11 and MYB31 from prior literature. 4. The COMT1-centered network contains multiple feed-forward loops, including KN1-GRF4-COMT1.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Late branch control point influencing lignin composition; key control role supported at network level
Pathway_Genes.Promoters: Native maize COMT1 promoter bait; 35S promoter for TF fusions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: CAGE; published ChIP-seq and RNA-seq integrated
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MYB100; KN1; FEA4; P1; MYB11; MYB31; GRF4; NAC62
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for lignin composition engineering and brown-midrib-related biomass quality improvement via regulatory manipulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Encoded by brown midrib3 (bm3) as noted in the paper.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursors for anthocyanins/phlobaphenes depending branch context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Anthocyanin biosynthetic target with differential combinatorial TF regulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD4 alone activated the A1 promoter in yeast. 2. Addition of NAC37 impaired LBD4-mediated A1 activation, contrasting with their cooperative requirement on Bz1. 3. Prior work discussed in the paper supports that C1 recruits R to the A1 promoter, explaining why R was not recovered for A1 in Y1H.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Directed Y1H/co-transformation assays in yeast; prior literature context
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Illustrates promoter-specific combinatorial control valuable for pigment engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was directly used in TF cooperation experiments.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Glycosylates anthocyanidins/flavonoids to more stable glucosides
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for stable anthocyanin end-product formation/accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Late anthocyanin gene showing strong combinatorial transcriptional regulation and extremely high TF connectivity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Bz1 promoter was recognized by 129 TFs, making it one of the most highly connected promoters. 2. ZHD14 and EREB12 were together required to activate Bz1 in yeast. 3. LBD4 and NAC37 were together required for activation of Bz1. 4. Y2H confirmed physical interactions for ZHD14-EREB12 and LBD4-NAC37 pairs. 5. MBF1.3 binding to Bz2 rather than Bz1 was validated, but Bz1 remained a major regulatory hub.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; combinatorial co-transformation yeast assays; Y2H for TF-TF interaction
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal modification step; key accumulation step for stable anthocyanins
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target promoter for engineering anthocyanin accumulation through combinatorial TF design
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: R factor was identified for Bz1 but not A1, consistent with direct DNA contact at Bz1.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in anthocyanin handling/sequestration rather than core scaffold biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/sequestration of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Anthocyanin pathway target promoter bound by MBF1.3 in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MBF1.3 was identified by Y1H as binding Bz2 promoter. 2. ChIP-qPCR confirmed MBF1.3 association with Bz2 in maize protoplasts.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory entry point for improving anthocyanin retention/accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper focuses on promoter regulation, not direct enzymology of Bz2.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB family; ortholog of Arabidopsis AtMYB58/AtMYB63
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAL1; PAL3; PAL4; PAL5; PAL6; PAL8; PAL9 and six additional phenolic gene promoters
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PAL4; broadly binds multiple PAL and other phenolic promoters
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functions as a positive regulator of maize phenolic metabolism and activates PAL4 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: High-connectivity activator coordinating expression of many phenolic genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB65 recognized 13 maize phenolic gene regulatory sequences, including seven PAL promoters. 2. ChIP-qPCR validated 10 of 13 Y1H interactions in maize protoplasts. 3. MYB65 strongly activated a PAL4::Luc reporter in maize protoplasts. 4. The paper concludes that MYB65 functions as a transcriptional activator in maize, consistent with AtMYB58/63 ortholog function.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; ChIP-qPCR; transient luciferase transactivation assay in maize protoplasts
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PAL1; PAL3; PAL4; PAL5; PAL6; PAL8; PAL9 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in network feed-forward motifs at pathway level; specific autoregulation not shown
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Protoplast assays from etiolated B73 × Mo17 F1 seedlings
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used for MYB65-GFP effector in validations
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: CAGE for promoter definition; no accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate activator for boosting broad phenylpropanoid flux in maize metabolic engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of 11 TFs recognizing 10 or more promoters.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: DNA binding/transcriptional regulation; activation or repression not resolved in this paper
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 24 phenolic gene promoters across phenylpropanoid and flavonoid pathways
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Broad promoter recognition; directional effect not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as the highest-connectivity TF in the identified phenolic GRN
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Potential coordinator of pathway-wide phenolic gene expression due to highest promoter connectivity
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD35 recognized 24 phenolic gene promoters, the highest number in the dataset. 2. It spans both phenylpropanoid and flavonoid branches. 3. Its biochemical directionality and in planta effects remain to be tested.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Network-level feed-forward motif potential not directly tested for LBD35
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-priority candidate master regulator for coordinated manipulation of maize phenolics
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions of LBD35 orthologs were unknown according to the paper.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: LBD/LOB domain family; orthologous context to Arabidopsis LBD16
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Likely transcriptional activation based on Arabidopsis ortholog context, but not directly tested here
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad high-connectivity regulator candidate in maize phenolic network
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Potential broad coordinator across phenolic branches
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD24 recognized 23 phenolic gene promoters. 2. It is among the most connected TFs discovered in the network. 3. The paper notes AtLBD16, its ortholog, acts as a transcriptional activator in lateral root formation, but direct metabolic regulatory activity in maize remains untested.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Phytohormone/developmental links inferred for LBD family; not directly tested
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for pathway-wide phenolic trait manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not directly functionally validated beyond promoter binding.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: DNA binding/transcriptional regulation; direction not tested
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High-connectivity regulator candidate across phenolic branches
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD20 bound 18 phenolic gene promoters. 2. It is one of the 11 high-connectivity TFs in the maize phenolic network. 3. Functional roles of LBD20 orthologs were unknown according to the paper.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for coordinated engineering of maize phenolics
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct in planta functional assay reported.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: DNA binding/transcriptional regulation; directional effect not resolved
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 15 promoters including HCT10; CCR3; 4CL3; C2 and other phenolic genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Broad promoter binding across all branches
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: A high-connectivity DOF regulator candidate spanning all major phenolic branches
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Likely important coordinator because it binds general phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DOF28 recognized 15 promoters from phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid pathways. 2. ChIP-qPCR validated DOF28 association with HCT10 and also detected association with 4CL3 and C2 despite these not being Y1H positives. 3. DOF28 is among the 11 TFs binding 10 or more phenolic promoters.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in network motifs by virtue of broad connectivity; not explicitly resolved
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for GFP-DOF28 construct in ChIP validation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Attractive target for coordinated engineering of lignin/flavonoid balance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ChIP-only positives illustrate complementarity of Y1H and in vivo assays.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: DNA binding/transcriptional regulation; direction not tested
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 13 promoters across all branches of the phenolic pathway
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad phenolic regulator candidate from a poorly characterized TF family
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Likely important because it spans all branches and binds many promoters
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CPP8 bound 13 promoters across all branches of the phenolic pathway. 2. This points to an unexpected role of CPP family proteins in metabolic regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Novel regulator family for phenolic engineering with potentially distinct control logic
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CPP family had limited prior functional characterization in plants.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Phenolic biosynthetic gene expression potentially linked to nitrogen status
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 13 promoters corresponding to all branches of phenolic biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suggests a previously unrecognized connection between nitrogen-response regulators and phenolic metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-linked coordinator integrating cellular nitrogen status with phenolic pathway expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NLP9 recognized 13 promoters across all branches of phenolic biosynthesis. 2. The paper interprets this as a possible link between phenolic biosynthesis and nitrogen cellular status.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Nitrogen/nitrate status inferred from NLP family biology
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Potential nitrate/nitrogen context inferred, not experimentally tested here
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could enable coupling of nutrient-use status with phenolic composition in breeding/engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Text first mentions NLP14 among top TFs but detailed results specify NLP9 with 13 promoters; NLP9 is retained here based on explicit detailed description.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 12 genes across all branches of the phenolic pathway
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broad regulator candidate across maize phenolic pathways
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. bZIP63 recognized regulatory regions of 12 genes across all branches of the phenolic pathway. 2. This places bZIP family members among major candidate coordinators of maize phenolics.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful candidate for broad transcriptional rewiring of phenolic traits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The introductory count section mentions bZIP13, but the detailed result text specifies bZIP63 recognizing 12 promoters; detailed explicit statement prioritized.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: General phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PAL9; 4CL3; ALDH1; ALDH5; COMT1; F5H1; HCT6; HCT10; FLS1; C2
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Broad promoter binding across pathway branches
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Multi-branch regulator candidate integrating control of early phenylpropanoid, lignin, and flavonoid genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: High-connectivity regulator spanning multiple branches and key lignin genes
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. C3H42 bound 10 different promoters in the phenolic pathway. 2. Targets included two general phenylpropanoid genes, multiple lignin genes, and two flavonoid genes. 3. This identifies C3H42 as a broad-spectrum regulator candidate for phenolic metabolism.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PAL9; 4CL3; ALDH1; ALDH5; COMT1; F5H1; HCT6; HCT10; FLS1; C2 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to simultaneously alter lignin and flavonoid traits
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Family members are often linked to development and stress in other plants.

Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: 10 phenolic biosynthesis gene promoters including Bz2 and PAL5
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promoter association validated in vivo for Bz2 and PAL5
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly associates with phenolic gene promoters and likely contributes to activation/co-activation of pathway gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important because it directly binds multiple pathway promoters despite MBF1 traditionally being viewed as co-activator
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MBF1.3 recognized 10 phenolic biosynthesis gene promoters in Y1H. 2. ChIP-qPCR validated in vivo interaction with Bz2 and PAL5 promoters. 3. The study supports that MBF1 family members can directly recognize target promoters in maize phenolic regulation.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: May bridge TBP and specific TFs based on family function; direct complex not tested here
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used for GFP-MBF1.3 effector in ChIP validation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Interesting noncanonical regulator for manipulating phenolic transcriptional outputs
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlights blurred distinction between TFs and co-regulators.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Lignin and general phenylpropanoid gene expression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Promoter binding; directionality not tested in reporter assay here
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: HCT6; COMT1; also associated with 4CL3 in ChIP but not Y1H; not FLS1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Binds specific phenolic promoters in vivo
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Validated regulator of lignin-related promoters and example of Y1H/ChIP complementarity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Demonstrates direct in vivo targeting of key lignin genes HCT6 and COMT1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB100 interacted with HCT6 and COMT1 promoters in Y1H. 2. ChIP-qPCR confirmed in vivo enrichment at HCT6 and COMT1 promoters. 3. ChIP also detected association with 4CL3 despite negative Y1H result, illustrating complementary detection by the two methods. 4. No interaction with FLS1 was detected by either assay.
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Possible indirect recruitment to 4CL3 suggested; not proven
Regulation_Genes.Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter used for GFP-MYB100 construct
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful regulator for targeting lignin branch genes with validated promoter occupancy
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as demonstration case for Y1H/ChIP validation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: With NAC37 activates Bz1; alone activates A1 better than with NAC37
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promoter-specific combinatorial regulator of anthocyanin pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Illustrates that anthocyanin gene regulation can depend on TF combinations and promoter context
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LBD4 and NAC37 were together required to activate the Bz1 promoter in yeast. 2. LBD4 alone was sufficient to activate the A1 promoter. 3. NAC37 impaired LBD4-mediated activation of A1. 4. LBD4 physically interacted with NAC37 in Y2H.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast co-transformation Y1H cooperation assay; yeast two-hybrid
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports combinatorial TF engineering for anthocyanin traits rather than single-factor deployment
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Strong example of promoter-dependent TF cooperation.

Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Combinatorial transcriptional control via TF partnership
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Required with LBD4 for Bz1 activation; interferes with LBD4 activation of A1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a context-dependent co-regulator in anthocyanin promoter control
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Demonstrates that NAC factors can tune promoter-specific anthocyanin regulation through interaction with LBD factors
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NAC37 cooperated with LBD4 to activate Bz1 in yeast. 2. NAC37 reduced LBD4-mediated activation of A1. 3. NAC37 physically interacted with LBD4 in Y2H.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Yeast co-transformation Y1H cooperation assay; yeast two-hybrid
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential context-tuning component for anthocyanin regulatory engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In planta role remains to be tested.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Requires EREB12 to activate Bz1 in yeast
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of a cooperative TF pair required for Bz1 promoter activation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Shows combinatorial control of a late anthocyanin gene
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZHD14 alone did not activate Bz1 in yeast. 2. ZHD14 together with EREB12 activated Bz1. 3. ZHD14 physically interacted with EREB12 in Y2H.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports multi-TF engineering strategies for pigment genes
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified via multi-plasmid yeast strain analysis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Requires ZHD14 to activate Bz1 in yeast
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Part of a cooperative TF pair needed for Bz1 activation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Demonstrates promoter activation requiring TF partnership
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EREB12 alone did not activate Bz1 in yeast. 2. EREB12 and ZHD14 together enabled Bz1 activation. 3. EREB12 physically interacted with ZHD14 in Y2H.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of inducible pigment control modules
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Example of unexpected advantage of transformation-based Y1H screen.
